title: Boat Rental in Boracay: Costs, Tips, and Best Routes for 2026
html:
Boracay offers some of the finest boating in the Philippines, with departures from Cagban Jetty Port and trips that cost from PHP 15,000 for a half-day motorboat hire. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Boracay or list your own.

Where to set off in Boracay

Most boat trips start from Cagban Jetty Port on the south side near Manoc-Manoc. From there you can sail to White Beach for swimming, Crystal Cove Island for snorkelling, or Puka Shell Beach on the north shore. Other real departure points include Tambisaan Beach on the east coast, accessible from Bulabog Beach after a short tricycle ride, and the private pier at Punta Bunga Cove for luxury yachts. Many itineraries also visit Crocodile Island and Balinghai Beach, both within 5 nautical miles of the main jetty. You can rent a boat in a few minutes.

What kind of boat should you rent?

  1. Fairline motor yacht, ideal for a full-day family outing with up to 10 guests, from PHP 25,000 per day. See the Fairline listing on BoatHire24.
  2. Azimut flybridge, perfect for corporate groups or celebrations carrying up to 12 guests, from PHP 40,000 per day.
  3. Lagoon catamaran, stable and spacious for up to 15 guests, from PHP 35,000 for a half-day charter.
  4. Axopar tender, agile for small groups of 4-5 guests exploring hidden coves, from PHP 12,000 for a half-day rental.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Boracay?

Prices vary by boat type and duration, with shared tours from PHP 1,500 per person and private charters from PHP 12,000. One option worth a look is the Fairline for hire.

  • Shared island-hopping tour: PHP 1,500 per person, includes lunch and snorkelling gear.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4-5 guests): from PHP 12,000.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 15 guests): from PHP 35,000.
  • Crewed yacht (up to 12 guests, full-day): from PHP 30,000.

These rates are typical for 2026. To compare verified boat listings, you can rent a boat directly through BoatHire24. You can also see more at check availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Boracay?

For uncrewed boat hire in the Philippines, you need a valid ICC or equivalent licence; crewed charters do not require one. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

During high season (November to May, especially the Amihan winds from December to February), book your boat at least one week in advance. The waters around Crocodile Island are calmer in the morning.

How does boat rental work in Boracay?

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward: you choose your dates, select a vessel, and pay securely online. The platform connects you directly with local operators. For a step-by-step guide, see how boat rental works.

At a glance

  • Price range: PHP 1,500, PHP 40,000 per trip
  • Peak months: November to May (Amihan wind)
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 4 to 15 guests depending on boat
  • Currency: Philippine Peso (PHP)

Questions about boat rental in Boracay

Can I rent a boat for a whole day in Boracay?

Yes, full-day charters are widely available from Cagban Jetty Port and other departure points, typically lasting eight hours.

Is snorkelling equipment provided on boat trips?

Most chartered boats include basic snorkelling gear, but you can bring your own for a better fit.

What is the best time of year for boating in Boracay?

The ideal boating season runs from November to May when the Amihan wind keeps seas calm and visibility high.

Can I captain the boat myself without a licence?

Only crewed charters are available without a licence; for self-drive you need a recognised ICC or Philippine equivalent.

If you own a boat in Boracay, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 trip. To get started, check availability on boathire24.com.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Boracay and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
